summary: Ukrainian forces have deployed E-300 drones, rebuilt from light aircraft, on the Zaporizhzhia front, marking their expanded use after earlier long-range strikes into Russia. In Kharkiv, FPV crews destroyed Ukrainian reconnaissance drones, while Russian operators using the "Yolka" anti-drone system downed Ukrainian fixed-wing "Hornet" strike drones. The "Rubikon" Center reported 136 targets eliminated on March 14, 2026, across Donbas, Sumy, and Krasny Liman. Ukrainian FPV drones struck the Afipsky oil refinery in Krasnodar Krai, causing a major fire. Footage shows Ukrainian forces using FPV drones to eliminate Russian infantry after surrender attempts failed, and Russian soldiers being hit by multiple simultaneous FPV strikes. Long-range fiber-optic drones continue to destroy Russian artillery positions, signaling the decline of tube artillery on the front.
